'''Lee Mossop''' (born 17 January 1989) is an English retired professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]]<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and the [[England Knights]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] over two separate spells in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Barrow Raiders]] and [[Workington Town]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the Super League. Mossop has also played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[NRL]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newsandstar.co.uk/sport/17957896.ex-hensingham-amateurs-go-head-head-super-league-grand-final/|title=Ex-Hensingham amateurs go head to head in Super League Grand Final|website=News and Star|date=9 October 2019 |language=en|access-date=2019-11-15}}</ref>

==Background==
Mossop was born in [[Whitehaven]], [[Cumbria]], England.

Lee attended [[St John Rigby College, Wigan|St John Rigby College]] in [[Orrell, Greater Manchester|Orrell]] from 2005 to 2007 and gained A-levels in subjects such as Physical Education and Business Studies.

In his youth he played for amateur side [[Hensingham ARLFC]].

==Playing career==
===2000s===
Mossop made his début in the [[Challenge Cup]] victory against [[Pia Donkeys]] on 20 April 2008.<ref name="Wigan Cruise to Victory Over Pia">{{cite web|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/news_full.asp?newsid=3795|title=Wigan Cruise to Victory Over Pia|publisher=St Helens|date=20 April 2008|accessdate=21 April 2008}} {{Dead link|date=October 2010|bot=H3llBot}}</ref> where he impressed scoring a try. He was involved in four games off the bench in total in 2008. He was given a three-year deal with Wigan in 2008.

In April 2009, Mossop was loaned to [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] following an appearance for Wigan during their [[2009 Challenge Cup]] fixture against Barrow.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nwemail.co.uk/raiders/barrow_raiders_sign_wigan_forward?referrerPath=5|title=Barrow Raiders Sign Wigan Forward|work=North West Evening Mail|accessdate=22 April 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605102758/http://www.nwemail.co.uk/raiders/barrow_raiders_sign_wigan_forward?referrerPath=5|archive-date=5 June 2011|url-status=dead}}</ref> He impressed at [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] making four appearances scoring a try.

In May 2009 it was announced that Lee would join [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] on a month loan, and it was subsequently announced that Lee would be staying at [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] on a season long loan with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] having the option to recall Lee at any time if needed.

In July 2009 it was announced that Lee would rejoin [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] from his loan spell at [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] due to injuries to captain [[Sean O'Loughlin]] and [[Harrison Hansen]]<ref>http://www.rleague.com/db/player/m/mossop_lee/index.php Mossop recalled to Wigan squad</ref> He went on to make 11 appearances that season, scoring 1 try.

===2010s===
Mossop started 2010 brightly in Wigan colours and attracted interest from Australian [[NRL]] side [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]]. 
[[File:Lee Mossop.JPG |thumb|right|Mossop playing for Wigan in 2010]]
However, on 29 March 2010 Mossop signed a five-year deal at the Wigan club which would see him stay as a Cherry and White until the end of the 2014 season.

Mossop started the 2011 season as a {{rlp|pr}} and impressed many in that position. He made 29 appearances for Wigan in 2011's [[Super League XVI]], scoring tries against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], Huddersfield and rivals [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in Wigan's 10–32 win at [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in June.

Mossop played in Wigan's [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]]. He came on off the bench before having to leave the field after dislocating his shoulder. Mossop still came back on at half time despite his dislocated shoulder and played a pivotal role in the scoring of [[Jeff Lima (rugby league)|Jeff Lima]]'s second try. Mossop helped Wigan towards their 18th Challenge Cup success.<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18-28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18-28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Brown|first=Oliver|title=Challenge Cup final 2011: Wigan's warrior spirit shades controversy in emphatic win over Leeds Rhinos|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8728278/Challenge-Cup-final-2011-Wigans-warrior-spirit-shades-controversy-in-emphatic-win-over-Leeds-Rhinos.html|accessdate=21 November 2019|newspaper=The Telegraph|date=28 August 2011}}</ref>

In October 2011, Mossop was announced in the 22-man squad for the England Knights, a team of emerging Super League players and future [[England Rugby League]] stars. He played in two games for the Knights against France and his home-county, [[Cumbria]], winning both of them.

In March 2012, England coach [[Steve McNamara]] announced his England Elite 32-man squad which Mossop was included in. This was Mossop's first inclusion in the England Rugby League first team squad.

He made his England first team début in the International origin game against the Exiles on 16 March 2012 where he came on off the bench. [[Josh Charnley]] and [[Carl Ablett]] also made their England débuts in this game.  He backed up this England début by being picked again for the second and final Exiles game of the series. He also came off the bench in this game.

Mossop earned his first 3 England caps in the 2012 Autumn International series against France and Wales which England won. He came off the bench for the first game and managed to earn a starting spot at prop in the following two games.

On 23 April 2013 [[Wigan Warriors]] and [[Parramatta Eels]] announced that Mossop would be joining the [[NRL]] competition for two years commencing 2014.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news-display/Lee-Mossop--Gareth-Hock-sign-with-the-Parramatta-Eels/73056 |title=Lee Mossop & Gareth Hock sign with the Parramatta Eels - Parramatta Eels |access-date=24 April 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130426004009/http://www.parraeels.com.au/news-display/Lee-Mossop--Gareth-Hock-sign-with-the-Parramatta-Eels/73056 |archive-date=26 April 2013 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

Before leaving Wigan he played in the [[2013 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]],<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23794947|title= Hull FC 0-16 Wigan Warriors|date=24 August 2013|work= BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref> and also in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref>

Mossop made his début for the [[Parramatta Eels]] on 6 June 2014 against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].  After the match Mossop stated that "The Aussies like to rate the NRL competition as the best in the world and they're probably not wrong", "It's a lot faster but a lot of people expect it to be harder but I think Super League is a more physical competition". On 8 July 2014, after a match against the [[Melbourne Storm]], it was revealed that Mossop would be out for the season after sustaining an injury to his left shoulder.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ny0xNjQ0LTQtLS0tLS04MDQxLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLQ==|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.leaguefreak.com/parramatta-eels-english-import-lee-mossop-claims-super-league-is-more-physical-than-the-nrl-7526/|title = Parramatta Eels English Import Lee Mossop Claims Super League is More Physical Than the NRL|date = 14 June 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web| url = http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/parramatta-eels-forward-lee-mossop-ruled-out-for-the-season-20140708-zt0ou.html| title = Parramatta Eels forward Lee Mossop ruled out for the season| date = 8 July 2014}}</ref>

A season later, Mossop went on to rejoin [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] after an injury hit stint in the NRL. During this second spell at Wigan he played in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|publisher=Guardian|date=10 October 2015|accessdate=8 November 2019}}</ref>

Mossop completed his move to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] on 3 November 2016. He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

On 17 October 2020, he played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat for Salford against Leeds at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 11 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Mossop was sent off in Salford's match against Leeds after punching [[Konrad Hurrell]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12343301/leeds-coach-richard-agar-pleased-after-eventful-win-against-salford/|title=Leeds coach Richard Agar pleased after 'eventful' win against Salford|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

On 9 August 2021 Mossop announced his immediate retirement as a rugby league player on medical advice. In the announcement Mossop said "... after seeing the surgeon last week, he has told me there are no more surgeries he can offer to help prolong my career anymore and instead, it will be one final operation to give me a better quality of life with my wife and young children".<ref>{{cite web |last1=Wallworth |first1=Luke |title=Captain Lee Mossop forced to retire through medical orders |url=https://www.salfordreddevils.net/captain-lee-mossop-forced-to-retire-through-medical-orders/ |website=Salford Red Devils |access-date=9 August 2021 |date=9 August 2021}}</ref>
